mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers discount rates and eases financial conditions, so subsequent S&P 500 returns over the next month are positive when the policy rate is declining on a rolling 3-month basis.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are not positive in more than 55% of months with a 3-month decline in FEDFUNDS, or if the rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent baseline signals deteriorating labor demand and slower earnings expectations, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next month once unemployment has increased materially from its 6-month average.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points above its 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 45% of cases, or if the average forward return after the trigger is not below the unconditional mean by at least 1 standard error.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized improvement in PAYEMS and RSAFS indicates broad demand growth and better near-term revenue conditions, so the S&P 500 should rise over the next quarter when payrolls and retail sales are both accelerating.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint positive-slope condition on PAYEMS and RSAFS fails to outperform each series alone, or if 60-day forward returns are not positive in at least 60% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO relative to recent history signals stronger production and inventory absorption, which supports equity prices over the next month through higher expected real activity and improved cyclical earnings.
failure_condition: Reject if positive monthly changes in INDPRO are not followed by above-baseline 20-day S&P 500 returns, or if the signal's Rank IC is below 0.03 for any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in HOUST indicates collapsing residential demand and tighter cyclical growth expectations, which should be followed by negative S&P 500 returns over the next week when housing starts break down abruptly.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 10% below its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if the downside housing-break trigger is followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of cases, or if the median 5-day return is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation is high and FEDFUNDS is still below the implied inflation pressure, real rates remain loose and the market later reprices policy tightening, creating negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation is above its 6-month trend while FEDFUNDS is below 2%
failure_condition: Reject if high-inflation/low-rate episodes do not produce negative 20-day forward returns more often than not, or if the interaction term adds no explanatory power over single-series effects.

mechanism_narrative: Stable or falling PAYEMS combined with low UNRATE preserves income growth and spending power, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next month in a resilient labor-market regime.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of nondecreasing PAYEMS and low UNRATE does not outperform either variable alone, or if 20-day returns are not positive in at least 55% of qualifying periods.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery, rising GDPC1 combined with improving RSAFS marks a real-demand rebound that should lift equities over the next quarter as revenue expectations and risk appetite reset upward.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous improvements in GDPC1 and RSAFS are not followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in most cases, or if the joint signal underperforms the unconditional benchmark over a 3-year rolling window.
